title: AnalystView
description: The ONLY thing the Technical Analyst model (GLM-4.7-Flash) is allowed to emit per candidate. Enforced by constrained decoding. It is trade_proposal.schema.json MINUS risk_flags and scores.risk_score: the analyst owns technical/market/news support and the bull/bear/uncertainty argument, but the grammar structurally OMITS the risk fields so the optimistic analyst physically cannot self-clear its own risk. The orchestrator merges this with an AuditorView (Qwen3.6-27B) to assemble the immutable trade_proposal.schema.json.

symbol.description: Must match a symbol in the resolved universe (universe.yaml).

bull_case.description: Strongest honest argument FOR, grounded in the given signals.

bear_case.description: Strongest honest argument AGAINST, written as if you wanted it rejected.

uncertainty.description: What would make this wrong? Assumptions, missing/stale data, invalidation conditions.

direction.description: Required only when action == enter. (short reserved; long-only in early phases.)

cited_numbers.description: Optional: indicator values the analyst referenced, echoed back for the orchestrator to re-check against the Signal Engine. A mismatch beyond tolerance rejects the proposal.

revisit.description: Optional re-look condition, emitted ONLY on a PASS where the setup is structurally sound but the entry is extended. A deterministic queue re-submits the name to this same cascade ONCE if/when the condition is met. Never emit on enter; never to rescue a weak setup. A malformed value is ignored downstream, never fatal.

price.description: The pullback/breakout price at which the name becomes attractive.

dir.description: The side the live price must reach the level from: 'below' = re-look on a pullback to price; 'above' = re-look on a move up through price.
